reasoning: The forecast for a European commitment to provide Ukraine with HIMARS or a substitute rocket artillery launcher by Dec 31, 2025 faces major political and logistical hurdles. The U.S. has halted HIMARS support (per March 2025 reports of the Trump administration suspending intelligence sharing and assistance), which likely implies the U.S. would block third-party transfers of M270s, MARS II, or HIMARS from European stocks. Furthermore, alternative systems face export restrictions: Israel (which makes PULS) has blocked lethal aid to Ukraine, and South Korea (Chunmoo) has strict export laws regarding active conflict zones. While France debuted its own unrestricted alternative, the ‘Foudre’, in June 2025, defense procurement takes time, and securing a formal commitment to provide a newly unveiled system before the end of 2025 will be very tight. Although the gaping capability hole provides strong European incentive to act, the actual ability to bypass U.S. and Israeli vetoes in the next 4.5 months is low. Therefore, the probability has been adjusted downward from the crowd consensus of 0.59 to 0.41.
